SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.dashboard

Record Class DashboardProperties

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Record
com.societegenerale.failover.dashboard.DashboardProperties
@ConfigurationProperties(prefix="failover.dashboard") public record DashboardProperties(boolean enabled, String basePath, DashboardProperties.Exposure exposure, DashboardProperties.Security security, DashboardProperties.History history, DashboardProperties.Health health) extends Record
Configuration for the embedded failover dashboard.

Secure-by-default: enabled defaults to false. Nothing is mapped or served until the consumer explicitly sets failover.dashboard.enabled=true in YAML. See the dashboard design document, section 1a.

The basePath is the single dedicated namespace under which every dashboard surface lives — the static UI (basePath/**) and the JSON API (basePath/api/**). Override it in YAML to relocate the whole dashboard: 

 failover:
   dashboard:
     enabled: true
     base-path: /ops/failover
It must be a dedicated, non-root prefix so the dashboard cannot collide with the consumer's own services: it must start with '/', must not be the root '/', and must not end with a trailing '/'. A misconfigured value fails the context fast with a clear message — the accepted value is then used verbatim by both the resource handler and the controller mapping, so the two surfaces can never drift apart.

P0/P1 scope: master switch, basePath, config view. Exposure, security, history and health-threshold properties land in later phases.

Author:
Anand Manissery

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static final record 
    DashboardProperties.Exposure
    What the dashboard exposes once enabled=true.
    static final record 
    DashboardProperties.Health
    Health-classification thresholds on the healthyRate (design doc §4.4): HEALTHY when rate >= degradedThreshold, DEGRADED when rate >= unhealthyThreshold, otherwise UNHEALTHY.
    static final record 
    DashboardProperties.History
    Opt-in server-side trend history (design doc §8 option B): a fixed-size in-memory ring buffer sampled on a schedule, exposed at /api/metrics/series.
    static final record 
    DashboardProperties.Security
    Access-control posture for the dashboard (design doc §9 gate 4).

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    DashboardProperties(boolean enabled, String basePath)
    Convenience constructor applying all defaults (used in tests/programmatic setup).
    DashboardProperties(boolean enabled, String basePath, DashboardProperties.Exposure exposure, DashboardProperties.Security security, DashboardProperties.History history, DashboardProperties.Health health)
    Canonical, binder-targeted constructor — validates the base path fail-fast.
    DashboardProperties(boolean enabled, String basePath, DashboardProperties.Health health)
    Convenience constructor with custom health, default exposure/security/history.

    • DashboardProperties

      @ConstructorBinding public DashboardProperties(@DefaultValue("false") boolean enabled, @DefaultValue("/failover-dashboard") String basePath, @DefaultValue DashboardProperties.Exposure exposure, @DefaultValue DashboardProperties.Security security, @DefaultValue DashboardProperties.History history, @DefaultValue DashboardProperties.Health health)

  • Method Details

    • toString

      public final String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this record class. The representation contains the name of the class, followed by the name and value of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      toString in class Record
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this object. The value is derived from the hash code of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      hashCode in class Record
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object o)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. The objects are equal if the other object is of the same class and if all the record components are equal. Reference components are compared with Objects::equals(Object,Object); primitive components are compared with '=='.
      Specified by:
      equals in class Record
      Parameters:
      o - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the o argument; false otherwise.
